comment: 58yo male, retired long-haul truck driver with 25 years documented occupational diesel-exhaust (DEE) exposure (1995-2020). No personal diesel-attributable malignancy diagnosis. IARC Group 1 carcinogen (Monograph 105, 2012) — lung-cancer primary endpoint with smoking-modified additive-to-multiplicative interaction; bladder cancer limited-suggestive. Engine fires RF-OCC-DIESEL-EXHAUST-PREVENTION → routes to 2-track PreventionPlan: (a) IND-OCC-DIESEL-EXHAUST-PREVENTION-INTENSIFIED-SURVEILLANCE — risk-adjusted LDCT chest (USPSTF-modified for cumulative DEE), smoking-cessation counseling, urinalysis q1-2y bladder surveillance; (b) IND-OCC-DIESEL-EXHAUST-PREVENTION-OBSERVATION — general-population USPSTF only. Validates v0.3 occupational batch-2 pilot. Anchored DIS-NSCLC + DIS-SCLC + DIS-UROTHELIAL.
